elements.sent-2411-001: We show that if there exists a knot in $S^3$ that admits purely cosmetic surgeries, then there exists a hyperbolic one with this property.
elements.sent-2411-002: For a knot $K\subset S^3$ and two different slopes $r,r'\in\mathbb{QP ^1$, the surgeries $S^3_r(K)$ and $S^3_{r' (K)$ are purely cosmetic if they are orientation-preservingly homeomorphic.}}
elements.sent-2411-003: The cosmetic surgery conjecture in $S^3$ states that there are no purely cosmetic surgeries if $K$ is nontrivial.
elements.sent-2411-004: Notably, the collective work of Ni-Wu \cite{ni2015cosmetic , Hanselman \cite{hanselman2022heegaard , Daemi-Eismeier-Lidman \cite{daemi2024filtered and others reduces the conjecture to the following special case.}}}
elements.sent-2411-005: To list a few, the conjecture holds for torus knots, cable knots \cite{tao2019cable , composite knots \cite{tao2022knots , knots up to $19$ crossings \cite{futer2024excluding , and hyperbolic knots with $r$ an exceptional slope \cite{ravelomanana2016exceptional}\cite[Remark~1.7]{daemi2024filtered .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-006: We do not attempt to give a complete survey and refer readers to \cite{daemi2024filtered for more information.}
elements.sent-2411-007: In this short note, we prove the following theorem.
elements.sent-2411-008: The proof is topological, and exploits existing results mentioned above.
elements.sent-2411-009: Let $X$ be the component of the JSJ decomposition of $S^3\backslash K$ adjacent to $K$ and let $V$ be $X$ with $K$ refilled in.
elements.sent-2411-010: Then $V\cong\#^n(S^1\times D^2)$ for some $n$, and $K$ as a knot in $V$ is null-homologous, hyperbolic, and admits two different hyperbolic surgeries that are orientation-preservingly homeomorphic.
elements.sent-2411-011: In particular, there exists infinitely many hyperbolic knots in $S^3$ that admit purely cosmetic hyperbolic surgeries.. \end{Thm.}
elements.sent-2411-012: I want to thank my advisor Ian Agol for his continued guidance, support, and many stimulating discussions.
elements.sent-2411-013: I also thank Yi Ni for helpful discussions.
elements.sent-2411-014: This work is partially supported by the Simons Investigator Award 376200.
elements.sent-2411-015: We take such $K$ so that the number of JSJ tori in the JSJ decomposition of its complement is minimal.
elements.sent-2411-016: It is well-known that torus knots admit no cosmetic surgery (for example, because they have nontrivial Alexander polynomial, cf.. Theorem~\ref{thm:strong ).}
elements.sent-2411-017: Thus, to prove the theorem, in view of \cite[Remark~1.7]{daemi2024filtered which says hyperbolic knots admit no exceptional cosmetic surgeries, it suffices to show that $S^3\backslash K$ has trivial JSJ decomposition.}
elements.sent-2411-018: Assume for contrary that $S^3\backslash K$ has nontrivial JSJ decomposition.
elements.sent-2411-019: Let $X$ denote the $3$-manifold piece in its JSJ decomposition that is adjacent to $K$.

elements.sent-2411-026: Here we do not orient knots, so for us $w(P)$ is always a nonnegative integer.

elements.sent-2411-169: We obtain infinitely many hyperbolic knots in $K'_n\subset S^3$ with purely cosmetic surgeries by performing $(1/n)$-surgeries on the components of $L$ as in the proof of Claim 5 (the infinitude follows from the fact that the hyperbolic volumes of $K_n'$ converge to that of $V\backslash K$ strictly from below as $n\to\infty$).
